WTI.data_anomaly: fast_info prev_close $101.62 (+0.5%) vs history prev_close $99.64 (+2.5%). Using fast_info as headline; history used for technicals only.

macro_summary: Markets closed mixed Monday with SPY down fractionally (-0.16%) at $631.97 but the broader picture remains under pressure — SPY is now down roughly 4% over the past week as the Iran-Hormuz crisis enters day 22 and the Google TurboQuant algorithm disruption narrative (day 5) continues to weigh on semiconductors. SOXX fell another 3.93% to $309.79, extending the structural break from last Thursday's -4.4% collapse. VIX sits at 30.61, down slightly on the day but still above 30 — a level that blocks ACCUMULATE signals for all watchlist tickers. Per Yahoo Finance, oil settled above $100/bbl for the first time since 2022, with the stock market sliding on combined geopolitical and macro pressure. The Fed, per Yahoo Finance, is holding rates in the 3.5%-3.75% range with one cut projected for 2026, locking in the higher-for-longer thesis that continues to compress technology multiples.
commodities_note: WTI shows a data conflict: fast_info reports the prior close at $101.62 (+0.5% to today's $102.13), while history data implies a prior close of $99.64 (+2.5%). Using the fast_info figure as the headline daily move (+0.5%); the 5-day and monthly trend is the more reliable signal here — WTI has surged roughly 10% over the past week, driven entirely by the Hormuz disruption now in week 3. Per Reuters, oil briefly touched $119/bbl intraday Monday before pulling back, reflecting the severity of the supply bottleneck. Gold hit $4,611.90 (+1.85%), continuing its safe-haven bid — it has now traded above $4,400 for over a week and is extended well above any reasonable near-term entry. The DXY firmed to 100.41 (+0.31%), reflecting the Fed's hawkish pause premium. No attractive entry exists for gold-linked instruments given the extreme extension from the 50-day average; GLD is effectively in price discovery.

D05_SI.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at HOLD. Would reconsider above SGD 57.60 with volume confirmation, or reassess below SGD 57.00 if support fails.

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

O39_SI.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at WATCH. Becomes actionable on a pullback to the 50-day average near SGD 21.18, or on a volume-confirmed break above SGD 23.03 resistance.

U11_SI.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. Would reconsider above SGD 37.42 (50-day average) with volume; structural support at SGD 35.57 (200-day) is the downside reference if current levels fail.

C6L_SI.signal_rationale:
Signal changed from WATCH to CAUTION — R:R is mathematically distorted (rr_distorted flag active) and analyst consensus projects a 64.3% earnings decline, which per the EARNINGS DECLINE PROMINENCE RULE must anchor this writeup.

Singapore Airlines faces a 64.3% expected earnings decline per analyst consensus — a material deterioration that overwhelms the modest technical improvement seen in recent sessions. The R:R calculation is flagged as distorted (resistance sits within 1% of current price at SGD 6.65 vs. current SGD 6.59), making any mechanical ratio meaningless for entry decisions. The Hormuz disruption is the structural driver: per The Wall Street Journal, rising Middle East conflict fears are pushing oil higher and squeezing airline margins through jet fuel costs. At a 17.3x forward PE with only 1.9% revenue growth, the valuation offers little cushion for an earnings contraction of this magnitude. FCF yield of 8.3% is the one redeeming metric, but that figure pre-dates the current fuel cost shock. Use SGD 6.34 support as the structural reference — that's 3.8% below current price and represents the first meaningful floor if sentiment deteriorates further.

Bottom line: SIA is in a difficult spot — earnings are expected to fall sharply while fuel costs keep rising. Avoid until the oil situation resolves or earnings guidance improves.

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

Z74_SI.signal_rationale:
Signal unchanged at WATCH. Singtel sits 1.9% above its rising 50-day average at SGD 4.88 with RSI at 49.6 — technically neutral, within the acceptable entry zone but not at an ideal level. The R:R is 2.0:1 to the nearest resistance at SGD 5.15, with invalidation at the 50-day average (SGD 4.88) — a tight stop given price is already close. Invalidation at SGD 4.88 (1.8% below) would confirm rally failure. Structural support sits at SGD 4.39 (11.7% below) as the longer-term floor. The valuation concern is significant: at 24.6x forward PE with a PEG of 3.8 and revenue growth of only 0.9%, Singtel trades at a 78.3% premium to the Singapore cluster median — expensive for what is essentially a utility-like telco. Analyst consensus is buy, but there are no earnings estimates available to anchor the growth story. Volume has been below average throughout the recent move, which is a meaningful caveat. Tight invalidation — normal intraday volatility may trigger stop before the thesis is tested. Use SGD 4.39 (11.7% below) as the mental stop for position sizing.

Bottom line: Singtel is expensive relative to Singapore peers given its modest growth rate. Wait for a pullback to SGD 4.88-4.90 and volume confirmation before entering.

NVDA.signal_rationale:
Signal unchanged at CAUTION. NVDA sits 9.8% below its declining 50-day average of $183.05 with RSI at 34.0 — oversold but not yet at a capitulation extreme. The R:R is flagged as distorted (rr_distorted active — invalidation at $164.30 is only 0.5% below current price of $165.17), making the mechanical 23.4:1 ratio meaningless. Tight stop — normal intraday volatility may trigger invalidation before the thesis is tested. Use structural support at $86.60 (47.6% below) as the absolute floor context, though the more relevant near-term reference is the $164.30 support zone. The thesis remains intact in the medium term: per NVDA's Q4 FY2026 earnings call, Blackwell Ultra (B300) enters volume production in Q2-Q3 2026 with Q1 FY2027 revenue guided to $78B. At 14.9x forward PE with a PEG of 0.71 and 73.2% revenue growth, the stock is actually the cheapest quality name in the semis cluster on a growth-adjusted basis — 18.1% discount to cluster median. However, VIX above 30 blocks ACCUMULATE, and the Google TurboQuant algorithm disruption (day 5) continues to cloud the hyperscaler capex thesis. Resistance at $184.47 and $194.67 are the levels to watch on any recovery attempt.

Bottom line: NVDA is genuinely cheap for its growth rate, but the market is pricing real uncertainty about whether AI infrastructure spending will be as GPU-intensive as expected. Wait for VIX to drop below 30 and price to reclaim $175-180 with volume before re-evaluating entry.

AMD.signal_rationale:
Signal unchanged at CAUTION. AMD's R:R is flagged as distorted (rr_distorted active — invalidation at $195.54 is only 0.3% below current price of $196.04), making the mechanical 38.7:1 ratio meaningless. Tight stop — normal intraday volatility will trigger invalidation before the thesis is tested. Use structural support at $158.03 (19.4% below) as the real mental stop for position sizing. Trading 8.1% below its declining 50-day average with RSI at 44.7, AMD is technically in a no-man's land — not oversold enough to be a contrarian buy, not at a support that would anchor a thesis. The fundamental case is compelling: 18.2x forward PE in line with cluster median, PEG of 0.60, and 34.1% revenue growth with a 217.1% earnings growth forecast. Per AMD's IR press release (February 24, 2026) and Meta's newsroom, the confirmed Meta $100B+ GPU supply deal (MI450-based, performance-based warrants for up to 160M shares) validates AMD as a credible NVIDIA alternative at hyperscale — first 1GW shipments H2 2026. But the Google TurboQuant disruption is now the dominant narrative, and until hyperscaler capex direction clarifies, even confirmed deals face sentiment headwinds. Per Motley Fool, H2 2026 next-generation chip launches could be the catalyst that re-rates the stock.

Bottom line: AMD has one of the most compelling confirmed catalysts on the watchlist (the Meta deal), and it's cheap for its growth rate. The problem is the entire GPU-demand narrative is under pressure right now — wait for the architecture uncertainty to resolve before entering.

INTC.entry_block: CAUTION: forward P/E of 42x (>40x). Requires explicit justification of why earnings growth supports this multiple — default to WATCH unless thesis is clearly articulated with specific catalysts and timeline.
INTC.signal_rationale:
Signal unchanged at CAUTION. Intel is the most structurally challenged name on the watchlist: -4.3% today, down 11.1% from its declining 50-day average of $46.31, with an uninspiring R:R of 0.2:1 (nearest resistance at $42.48 vs. invalidation at $34.89). Revenue is declining (-4.1% YoY) and FCF yield is negative at -2.18% — the company is consuming cash rather than generating it. The entry block (soft CAUTION) flags a 41.6x forward PE against negative revenue growth and no consensus earnings growth estimate — a combination that is difficult to justify. The 200-day SMA at $34.89 (15.3% below) is the structural support floor; a break there would be technically severe. The CHIPS Act 18A foundry roadmap and High-NA EUV ramp (per ASML Q4 2025 call) remain the medium-term thesis, but with earnings on April 24 (24 days) and no earnings growth visibility, near-term binary risk is high. Intel's capex was cut approximately 25% per ASML's earnings commentary, which is not what a turnaround looks like.

Bottom line: Intel is expensive for a company with shrinking revenue and negative free cash flow. Avoid until the 18A foundry progress is confirmed in actual revenue, and watch the April 24 earnings as the first real signal of whether the turnaround is on track.

ASML.signal_rationale:
Signal unchanged at CAUTION. ASML fell 3.3% today and is 10.1% below its declining 50-day average of $1,395.28 — a sustained breakdown from a name that was a HOLD just a week ago. RSI at 37.3 is approaching oversold territory, and above-average volume on down days is a negative signal. The R:R is unfavourable at 0.7:1 (nearest resistance $1,407.54 is 12.2% above, but the 200-day SMA at $1,041.66 is 16.9% below — a significant downside if support fails). The PEG of 2.2 flags ASML as expensive for its current growth rate (4.9% revenue growth), though the medium-term story — record orders per the alphaspread.com article, AI-driven EUV demand, and the $71B 2030 revenue target — remains structurally intact. The near-term headwind is the Google TurboQuant disruption threatening the hyperscaler capex thesis that underpins ASML's order book. Earnings on April 15 (15 days) is the key catalyst that could either confirm the demand story or force a guidance reset — too close to enter ahead of a binary event. Structural support at $1,008.68 (19.6% below) is the worst-case reference.

Bottom line: ASML is a high-quality franchise at a valuation that's becoming more reasonable, but with earnings in two weeks and the capex thesis under pressure, patience is the right call. Watch the April 15 earnings as the potential entry signal.

MU.signal_rationale:
Signal unchanged at CAUTION — and the 20.4% drop in the past 5 days (including today's -8.7%) demands explanation. Micron now sits 20.4% below its 50-day average of $404.41, triggering the extreme extension cap (below SMA50, not above — this is a crash, not a rally). RSI at 31.6 is approaching oversold but hasn't hit the contrarian threshold. The dominant narrative is Google's TurboQuant algorithm, which per Simply Wall St. (March 31) aims to improve memory efficiency for AI models — prompting investors to question long-term memory demand even as Micron's full-year HBM4 capacity is sold out under binding contracts and the company has signed its first five-year customer agreement. That's a critical divergence: the OPERATIONAL picture (sold-out HBM4, five-year contracts) is bullish, but the ARCHITECTURE question (if TurboQuant reduces memory requirements per GPU, future demand growth slows) is what the market is pricing. Valuation anomalies: reported PEG of 0.26 with 756% earnings growth forecast — these figures look anomalous and likely reflect the cyclical nature of memory pricing rather than sustainable compounding; treat with caution. FCF yield of 0.8% is thin. The R:R is 1.8:1 using derived levels (not observed swing points), and rr_quality is 'derived' — insufficient for ACCUMULATE. 200-day SMA at $239.41 (25.6% below) is the structural support floor.

Bottom line: Micron has genuine near-term demand (HBM4 sold out, five-year contracts), but the market is asking a harder question about whether AI will need as much memory as previously thought. Worth watching closely, but not actionable until the TurboQuant impact is better understood.

AMZN.signal_rationale:
Signal unchanged at CAUTION. Amazon rose 1.2% today — a minor bounce within a broader downtrend, with price 7.2% below its declining 50-day average of $216.45 and RSI at 39.8. The R:R is actually favourable at 4.6:1 (upside to $219.43 resistance vs. invalidation at $196.93 support), but caution_source is base_scorer and VIX above 30 blocks ACCUMULATE. Per Barchart (March 30), Amazon lost a key AI chip executive, raising questions about team stability in a competitive AI infrastructure buildout — a soft negative but not a thesis-breaker given AWS's scale. FCF yield of 1.1% is thin, reflecting the massive $200B AI capex commitment (per AMZN Q4 2025 earnings call and 10-K filing) that is compressing near-term free cash flow. PEG of 1.92 and 13.6% revenue growth at 21.4x forward PE put AMZN in line with the BigTech cluster median. The structural thesis — AWS acceleration, AI monetization — remains intact, but the capex-to-earnings timing question is unresolved. Structural support at $161.38 (19.7% below) is the downside anchor.

Bottom line: Amazon is fairly priced for its growth rate and the AWS story is intact, but you're paying for earnings that won't materialise until the capex cycle matures. Wait for RSI to stabilise above 45 and price to reclaim $210-215 before revisiting.

GOOG.signal_rationale:
Signal unchanged at HOLD. Google sits at the intersection of the two biggest narratives this week: it's the author of TurboQuant (the algorithm threatening GPU-centric AI architecture) and a beneficiary of reduced memory costs if it succeeds. RSI at 24.0 is deeply oversold — one of the most extreme readings on the watchlist — and the stock is 12.5% below its declining 50-day average of $312.12, sitting just 3.7% above its 200-day SMA of $263.43, which is the structural support floor. The R:R is 1.9:1 (upside to $291.53 resistance, invalidation at 200-day $263.43) using observed levels — not quite at the 2.0:1 threshold for ACCUMULATE, and VIX above 30 would block it anyway. Per Motley Fool (March 31), a Motley Fool piece compares Alphabet vs. Amazon as the better AI buy — context suggesting institutional interest despite the sell-off. The social media addiction lawsuit verdict (per Yahoo Finance, March 29) is a longer-term legal overhang. At 20.3x forward PE and 18% revenue growth, PEG of 2.24 flags it as slightly expensive for current growth, but with 31.1% earnings growth expected, the forward picture is more attractive. The TurboQuant irony: if Google's own algorithm reduces AI infrastructure costs, Google Cloud and ad revenue benefit even as GPU names suffer.

Bottom line: Google is deeply oversold and close to a major support level, with one of the most asymmetric setups on the watchlist if the 200-day average holds. The catch is that the market is still pricing in worst-case scenarios. Wait for the 200-day average at $263.43 to confirm support before entering.

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

LITE.signal_rationale:
Signal unchanged at CAUTION. Lumentum fell 6.1% today and has dropped 10.2% over the past 5 days — a sharp reversal from a name that was extended 12.1% above its 50-day average before the Google TurboQuant disruption hit. The caution_source is hard_block (RSI extension combined with 202 consecutive sessions above the 50-day SMA) — this cannot be overridden. PEG of 0.13 with 65.5% revenue growth appears anomalous — verify earnings growth estimate before citing as a valuation argument; the trailing PE of 188.7x vs. forward PE of 43.7x implies a dramatic expected earnings inflection that the market is now questioning. FCF yield is effectively zero (-0.05%) — the thesis depends entirely on future growth. The NVIDIA $2B strategic investment (confirmed via NVIDIA investor.nvidia.com press release, March 2, 2026) and S&P 500 inclusion are structural tailwinds, but both are already known and priced. The TurboQuant disruption is the direct headwind — if hyperscaler AI builds shift toward algorithmic efficiency, the AI optics buildout timeline extends. Support at $528.01 (19.4% below) is the near-term floor to watch.

Bottom line: Lumentum has real strategic backing from NVIDIA, but it's a story stock with near-zero free cash flow at 43x forward earnings — and the market is now questioning how fast the AI optics buildout happens. Avoid until price pulls back to the 50-day average near $584 and the TurboQuant impact on optics demand is clearer.

COHR.signal_rationale:
Signal unchanged at CAUTION. Coherent dropped 9.2% today — one of the worst single-day moves on the watchlist — and has fallen 13.9% over five sessions. It's now 7.4% below its 50-day average of $237.14 with RSI at 40.8. Per Zacks (March 30), COHR and NVIDIA announced a multiyear silicon photonics partnership with a confirmed $2B investment (per NVIDIA newsroom press release, March 2, 2026 and Bloomberg) — the operational catalyst is real and confirmed. But the R:R is unfavourable at 0.6:1: nearest resistance at $247.15 (12.5% upside) is overwhelmed by invalidation at $175.24 (20.2% downside). The PEG of 0.05 is anomalous — with a trailing PE of 217.5x and forward PE of 29.6x, this PEG figure appears to reflect a dramatic expected earnings jump rather than current earnings quality; treat with caution. FCF yield of 0.32% is negligible. The 73% earnings growth forecast is the genuine driver, but the TurboQuant disruption clouds the timeline for AI optics demand. Volume was below average today despite the large drop — which could mean institutional conviction isn't fully behind the sell-off, but it doesn't change the technical picture. Support at $175.24 then $167.50 are the downside anchors.

Bottom line: Coherent has a confirmed NVIDIA partnership, but it's losing money on a cash basis and the R:R is unfavourable here. Watch for the stock to find a floor near $200-210 with volume before reconsidering.

XLE.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at HOLD. XLE is hard-blocked from entry (+12.8% above 50-day SMA with RSI at 76.7 — extreme overbought). The entry_block is firm and cannot be overridden. The Hormuz disruption provides the structural driver, but RSI at 76.7 makes this a momentum chase at a dangerous level. Would reconsider if XLE pulls back to the $54-55 range (near SMA50 support at $54.91) and RSI cools below 60.

PLTR.entry_block: CAUTION: forward P/E of 74x (>40x). Requires explicit justification of why earnings growth supports this multiple — default to WATCH unless thesis is clearly articulated with specific catalysts and timeline.
PLTR.signal_rationale:
Signal unchanged at CAUTION. Palantir fell 2.7% today, down 14.5% over five sessions, now 7.2% below its declining 50-day average of $148.18 with RSI at 38.7. The valuation remains the central problem: 73.7x forward PE represents a 244.4% premium to the BigTech cluster median of 21.4x — an extreme valuation that requires flawless execution and sustained hypergrowth to justify. The confirmed $10B Army IDIQ framework ceiling (not firm revenue) and $448M Navy ShipOS deal (per DoD press release) plus Rosenblatt's $200 price target are real catalysts, but IDIQ contracts are ceiling amounts — actual revenue could be significantly lower. The 2026 revenue guide of $7.18-7.20B (+61% YoY, per company guidance) is genuinely strong, and defense AI demand is structurally insulated from the TurboQuant disruption. The R:R is 4.4:1 using observed levels (invalidation $126.30, target $187.08), which is attractive in isolation — but VIX above 30 blocks ACCUMULATE and the 73.7x forward PE fails the ACCUMULATE valuation gate. FCF yield of 0.38% is negligible at current prices. Support at $126.30 then $119.17 are the downside floors. Per Investor's Business Daily (March 31), Palantir is cited as a key loser alongside Micron, Nvidia, and Tesla.

Bottom line: Palantir has real defense AI momentum and a growth rate that partially justifies its premium, but 74x earnings is difficult to defend in a risk-off environment with VIX above 30. Wait for the valuation to compress closer to 50x forward (roughly $94-95) or for a VIX normalization below 22 before reconsidering.

VUAA_L.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at HOLD. VUAA_L sits 5.9% below its declining 50-day average with RSI at 29.2 (oversold) — technically distressed but showing no reversal signal. Would reconsider on a break above $130.24 resistance with volume; structural support at $118.86 (3.6% below) is the downside reference if $121.62 fails.

singapore.summary: The Singapore cluster remains defensively positioned, with NIM compression now in its 21st consecutive cautious session and no reset signal in sight — US10Y at 4.34% is still well above the 4.20% threshold the market needs to see for bank re-rating. The Hormuz disruption is the new cluster-specific headwind: Singapore Airlines (C6L_SI) faces direct jet fuel cost pressure, which is why the signal was downgraded to CAUTION today despite the modest technical improvement seen last session. OCBC (O39_SI) and Singtel (Z74_SI) remain on WATCH but neither offers an attractive entry at current RSI levels.
singapore.thesis_status: Structurally impaired — NIM compression + Hormuz fuel shock
singapore.key_development: C6L_SI downgraded to CAUTION as 64.3% expected earnings decline combines with Hormuz-driven jet fuel cost surge, removing the WATCH signal assigned Monday.

semiconductors.summary: SOXX fell another 3.93% to $309.79 today — down 10.4% in five sessions and 18.5% from the pre-TurboQuant level — confirming the structural break is not a one-day event. All five semis tickers remain on CAUTION, with MU leading the downside (-8.7% today, -20.4% over five days) as the memory-efficiency narrative directly targets its core business model. The critical distinction in the cluster today: MU's operational picture (HBM4 sold out, five-year binding contracts) is bullish, but the architectural question (does TurboQuant reduce per-GPU memory requirements?) is what SOXX is pricing. NVDA at 14.9x forward PE and AMD at 18.2x forward PE are the cheapest they've been in years on a growth-adjusted basis — the fundamentals are compelling but the VIX above 30 and architecture uncertainty block accumulation.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Structural break sustained — TurboQuant disruption day 5
semiconductors.key_development: MU sold out of HBM4 on five-year binding contracts per Simply Wall St., yet fell 8.7% — the market is pricing future demand risk, not current demand reality.

big_tech.summary: The BigTech cluster shows the sharpest internal divergence on the watchlist: GOOG at RSI 24.0 (deeply oversold, near 200-day SMA support) vs. PLTR at 73.7x forward PE (still expensive despite the 14.5% five-day drawdown). AMZN's minor bounce (+1.2%) is noise within a downtrend. The TurboQuant announcement creates a paradox for GOOG specifically — as the algorithm's author, Google benefits from reduced infrastructure costs even as it pressures the broader tech capex narrative.
big_tech.thesis_status: Mixed — GOOG contrarian opportunity building, PLTR valuation still stretched
big_tech.key_development: GOOG RSI at 24.0 with price only 3.7% above 200-day SMA support creates the most asymmetric setup in the cluster, though VIX above 30 prevents action today.

photonics.summary: Both photonics names suffered sharp losses today (LITE -6.1%, COHR -9.2%), compounding last week's double-digit drops. The NVIDIA $2B investments in each (confirmed via NVIDIA press releases, March 2, 2026) provide structural support to the long-term thesis, but the TurboQuant disruption has introduced a timeline uncertainty — if AI builds shift toward algorithmic efficiency, the optics buildout happens over years, not quarters. Neither name has found a floor yet, and both remain hard-blocked or base-scored CAUTION.
photonics.thesis_status: Disrupted — TurboQuant extends the timeline uncertainty
photonics.key_development: COHR's confirmed NVIDIA silicon photonics partnership (per Zacks, March 30) failed to prevent a 9.2% drop — signals the market is discounting near-term demand regardless of strategic backing.

energy.summary: XLE is hard-blocked from entry at RSI 76.7 and 12.8% above its 50-day average — extreme overbought despite the legitimate Hormuz supply catalyst. The energy sector declined on Monday afternoon per MT Newswires despite WTI holding above $100, suggesting some profit-taking at overbought levels. The Hormuz crisis remains the only sector with a clear structural tailwind.
energy.thesis_status: Overbought — blocked entry despite valid catalyst
energy.key_development: WTI briefly touched $119/bbl intraday per Reuters before pulling back to $102, signaling markets are testing but not fully pricing a prolonged supply disruption at peak levels.

index.summary: VUAA_L at RSI 29.2 (oversold) and 5.9% below its declining 50-day average reflects the cumulative damage from the Iran-Hormuz crisis, TurboQuant disruption, and Fed hawkish pause — three simultaneous structural headwinds. No reversal signal is building despite extreme oversold conditions, consistent with the heuristic that VIX above 30 without breadth confirmation signals continuation risk.
index.key_development: VUAA_L has now been below its 50-day average for 22 consecutive sessions without a sustained bounce attempt — regime-level weakness per the cluster CAUTION heuristic.

interconnected.name: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSMC)
interconnected.reason: Primary fabricator for NVDA Blackwell Ultra and AMD MI450 — if either ramp accelerates, TSMC utilisation rates and ASPs move directly.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if TSM ADR pulls back 8-10% from current levels toward the $150-155 range, or revisit after NVDA's Q1 FY2027 earnings in May for Blackwell ramp confirmation.

interconnected.name: Broadcom
interconnected.reason: Custom ASIC and networking silicon supplier to hyperscalers — directly exposed to the same capex cycle as AMZN and GOOG, making it a read-through for the TurboQuant architectural disruption impact on silicon demand.
interconnected.entry_note: Becomes interesting if AVGO pulls back 10-12% to the $160-165 range where the 200-day SMA provides structural support; revisit after April earnings for hyperscaler custom ASIC order confirmation.

interconnected.name: Marvell Technology
interconnected.reason: Custom AI ASIC and high-speed interconnect supplier — if TurboQuant accelerates the shift from general-purpose GPUs toward custom silicon, Marvell is a direct beneficiary competing with NVDA's ecosystem.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if MRVL pulls back to the $60-65 range (approximately 15% below recent levels) with a stabilisation in SOXX above $320; a stronger-than-expected hyperscaler custom chip order in Q2 would be the trigger.

active_risks: Iran-Hormuz conflict (day 22): Strait near-halted at 2 vessels vs. 138 baseline; WTI above $100 with intraday spike to $119; per Reuters, hundreds of ships anchored on both sides awaiting resolution
Google TurboQuant algorithm (day 5): Architecture disruption risk to GPU-centric AI infrastructure; SOXX down 10.4% in five sessions; hyperscaler capex thesis under existential pressure
Fed hawkish pause: Rates held at 3.5%-3.75% with one cut projected for 2026 per Yahoo Finance; US10Y at 4.34%; higher-for-longer locks in tech multiple compression

US-China tariff reshaping: Section 122 surcharges (15%, 150 days) following Supreme Court IEEPA ruling; China 34% retaliation + export controls; supply chain onshoring thesis intact but demand uncertainty elevated
geopolitical.market_impact: The Hormuz crisis is directly hitting C6L_SI (fuel cost pressure contributing to the 64.3% earnings decline forecast) and supporting XLE above $61 — but XLE is overbought at RSI 76.7 and blocked from entry. The TurboQuant disruption is the proximate cause of SOXX's 10.4% five-session collapse, with MU (-20.4% in five days) and COHR (-13.9%) bearing the most acute pain despite confirmed NVIDIA partnerships and sold-out HBM4 capacity.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: Per Reuters (March 30), WTI briefly hit $119/bbl intraday — the highest since 2022 — before pulling back; per Investor's Business Daily (March 31), Dow Jones futures fell after comments from both Trump and Fed Chair Powell, with Micron, Nvidia, Palantir, and Tesla cited as key losers.
geopolitical.portfolio_action: No new entries while VIX remains above 30 — all ACCUMULATE gates are blocked; focus on monitoring GOOG near its 200-day SMA ($263.43) and MU's HBM demand narrative for the first signals of a potential re-entry setup.

events_this_week.event: CPI Report (March) — key test of whether the Fed's higher-for-longer thesis softens; a print below 2.7% core could reset rate-cut probabilities and lift tech multiples across the semis and BigTech clusters

MAS Monetary Policy Statement (April) — critical for Singapore cluster (D05_SI, O39_SI, U11_SI) given 21 consecutive sessions of NIM compression; any dovish surprise could be the NIM reset signal the cluster needs
ASML Q1 2026 Earnings — could confirm or reset the hyperscaler capex thesis; order book data will be the first hard datapoint on whether TurboQuant is changing customer behaviour
Intel (INTC) Q1 2026 Earnings — first read on 18A foundry progress and whether negative revenue growth trend is inflecting; forward PE of 41.6x with negative revenue growth makes this a binary event

Alphabet (GOOG) Q1 2026 Earnings — most anticipated earnings on the watchlist given TurboQuant disclosure and RSI at 24.0; capex guidance and TPU v7 commentary will resolve the architecture disruption uncertainty

portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: VIX above 30 blocks all ACCUMULATE signals; the simultaneous Hormuz oil shock, TurboQuant architecture disruption, and Fed hawkish pause create three independent headwinds that have pushed 10 of 17 tickers to CAUTION with no near-term entry opportunities.

watch_for_entry.note: Rising 50-day SMA but RSI 64.5 — slightly extended. Entry at SGD 21.18 (50-day SMA pullback) or volume break above SGD 23.03.
